{{Short description="Particle smaller than an atom; includes elementary particles and composite particles}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Subatomic particle
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Particle physics]], [[Elementary particle]], [[Quark]], [[Lepton]], [[Neutrino]]
}}

In [[physics]], a '''subatomic particle''' is a particle smaller than an [[atom]]. The two main categories are [[elementary particle]]s (not composed of other particles) and [[composite particle]]s (bound states of two or more elementary particles).

== Classification ==

=== Elementary particles ===
{{Main|Elementary particle}}
Fundamental particles with no known substructure:
* [[Fermion]]s — matter particles ([[quark]]s, [[lepton]]s)
* [[Boson]]s — force carriers ([[photon]], [[gluon]], [[W and Z bosons]], [[Higgs boson]])

=== Composite particles ===
Particles composed of elementary particles:
* '''[[Hadron]]s''' — bound by the [[strong interaction]]
  * [[Baryon]]s — three quarks ([[proton]], [[neutron]])
  * [[Meson]]s — quark–antiquark pairs ([[pion]], [[kaon]])
* '''[[Atomic nucleus|Atomic nuclei]]''' — protons and neutrons bound by the [[nuclear force]]
* '''[[Atom]]s''' — nucleus plus [[electron]]s

== History of discovery ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Year !! Discovery
|-
| 1897 || [[Electron]] — first subatomic particle identified ([[J. J. Thomson]])
|-
| 1911 || [[Atomic nucleus]] ([[Ernest Rutherford]])
|-
| 1919 || [[Proton]]
|-
| 1932 || [[Neutron]] ([[James Chadwick]])
|-
| 1932 || [[Positron]] — first [[antiparticle]]
|-
| 1956 || [[Neutrino]] (experimental detection)
|-
| 1968 || Evidence for [[quark]]s (deep inelastic scattering)
|-
| 1979 || [[Gluon]] (indirect evidence at [[DESY]])
|-
| 2012 || [[Higgs boson]] ([[Large Hadron Collider]])
|}

== Properties ==
Subatomic particles are characterised by:
* [[Mass]]
* [[Electric charge]]
* [[Spin (physics)|Spin]]
* [[Colour charge]] (quarks and gluons)
* Lifetime (stable or unstable)
* [[Antiparticle]] partner

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Lepton]]
* [[Neutrino]]
* [[Particle physics]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Standard Model]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Subatomic particles| ]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
